title: castellia tuberculosa
intro: Castellia tuberculosa, also known as castor oil plant, is a perennial herb native to tropical Africa and Asia. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly the seeds, which contain ricin, a toxic compound, but also ricinoleic acid, an anti-inflammatory and laxative agent. The seeds of Castellia tuberculosa have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and inflammation, due to its medicinal properties. The herbal preparation of the plant is typically in the form of oil, extracted from the seeds, which is used as a laxative, anti-inflammatory, and skin toner.
